story_title: Tenant Module Enablement and Role Permissions

requirements.requirement: Tenant administrators can enable ThinkTank so authorized tenant users can see and open the module.

tests.name: enables ThinkTank and emits thinktank.module.enabled with changed setting details
allows access only when tenant config is enabled and the role is bound
returns the authorized ThinkTank access payload and emits opened audit
shows ThinkTank navigation for admin, consultant, and client_pm users
shows ThinkTank navigation for respondent users when tenant config binds the role

requirements.requirement: Module configuration screen shows module status, role binding, retention policy, privacy confirmation, and latest audit summary.

tests.name: creates a tenant default config as disabled with empty roles and 90-day retention
wraps GET module config in a ThinkTank-owned data envelope
forwards GET module config with the request authorization header
renders module status, role binding, retention, privacy confirmation, and audit summary
shows an audit-delay fallback when latest audit summary is empty
unwraps nested data envelopes when loading module config

heuristics.error_path_coverage: audit delay and client error message paths present

requirements.requirement: Enabling ThinkTank emits thinktank.module.enabled with actor, tenant, timestamp, and changed setting.

tests.name: enables ThinkTank and emits thinktank.module.enabled with changed setting details

requirements.requirement: Disabled tenants receive a clear unavailable message when any tenant user opens ThinkTank.

tests.name: denies disabled tenant config with a distinct disabled message
blocks disabled tenants with a distinct disabled-state message
renders a clear disabled tenant state
hides ThinkTank navigation after backend reports the tenant module is disabled

requirements.requirement: Disabled tenants cannot create advisory sessions; this story introduces the reusable availability guard before sessions exist.

tests.name: exposes a reusable disabled-module guard for future session creation paths
reports disabled access distinctly from role denial

heuristics.endpoint_coverage: not applicable; reusable service guard only

requirements.requirement: Disabling ThinkTank emits thinktank.module.disabled with actor, tenant, timestamp, and changed setting.

tests.name: disables ThinkTank and emits thinktank.module.disabled
requires confirmation before disabling ThinkTank

heuristics.endpoint_coverage: covered through PUT path and service audit tests

requirements.requirement: Role access updates use existing CSAAS RBAC roles and take effect without a separate ThinkTank account model.

tests.name: rejects roles outside the existing CSAAS RBAC model
wraps PUT module config in a data envelope and uses CurrentTenant/CurrentUser
denies users whose role is not bound in the enabled tenant config
denies missing role values with explicit reason
hides ThinkTank navigation for respondent users when backend denies tenant access
shows ThinkTank navigation for respondent users when tenant config binds the role
treats every CSAAS role as a backend-check candidate

heuristics.authz_coverage: positive and negative role paths present
heuristics.error_path_coverage: invalid role and missing role paths present

tests.name: emits thinktank.role_access.updated when allowedRoles changes

endpoint_inventory.evidence: backend controller tests and frontend access client tests
backend admin controller tests and frontend proxy route tests
backend admin controller tests, service audit tests, frontend proxy route tests, and admin page save tests

recommendations.action: Add stable Playwright E2E coverage once shared authenticated admin fixtures exist.
Run bmad-testarch-test-review if the team wants a separate test-quality audit.
